summary: Energetic Broken Arrow, Row Jimmy and soaring second‑set jams
blurb: The show delivers strong versions of Broken Arrow, Row Jimmy and a vibrant China>Rider sequence, capped by an uplifting Knockin' On Heaven's Door encore. Jerry’s solos are on fire while Vince adds polished keys and flute touches. The crystal‑clear SBD capture makes this a recommended listen.
review: March 9 1993 at Rosemont Horizon finds the Dead in fine spring‑tour form. The first set rolls through new material with a particularly tasty Broken Arrow and Row Jimmy, both highlighted by Jerry’s soaring lead lines and Vince’s tasteful flute solo. A standout moment arrives in the second set when China Cat Sunflower segues into I Know You Rider, launching a bright, improvisational jam that flows straight into a powerful Playin' In The Band, followed by an inspired drum‑and‑space stretch before The Last Time and Black Peter close the set. The night ends on a high note with Sugar Magnolia and a heartfelt Knockin' On Heaven's Door encore. Across three recordings the consensus is clear: the band was tight, the energy lifted in the second half, and the Miller soundboard tape captures it all with crystal‑clear fidelity that overcomes Rosemont’s notoriously tricky acoustics.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings use positive language such as “crystal‑clear,” “solid,” “energetic,” and praise standout songs (Broken Arrow, Row Jimmy, China>Rider, Playin' In The Band, Knockin' On Heaven's Door). Only one audience tape notes poor sound quality and a few weak moments; the overall sentiment is strongly positive across recordings.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crystal‑clear mix, minimal noise and full coverage of the show’s highlights.

key_highlights: Standout performances of Broken Arrow, Row Jimmy and Knockin' On Heaven's Door.
Energetic China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider jam leading into a vibrant Playin' In The Band.
Solid second‑set flow with Black Peter and Sugar Magnolia delivering strong finishes.
Miller SBD capture offers superb audio quality that overcomes the venue’s acoustics.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers energetic, soaring solos on Broken Arrow, Row Jimmy and The Last Time; vocal presence strong throughout.
band_performance.Phil: Provides solid, supportive bass work, especially evident during the Tight Broken Arrow groove.
band_performance.Bob: Redemptive rhythm guitar on Sugar Magnolia and contributes to the overall drive of the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Locks in with Bill, adding steady rhythmic depth that underpins the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming throughout; shines during Playin' In The Band and the ensuing jam.
